Bologna Information System Catalog (Offline Mock)

This is a complete offline fixture to test the BeautifulSoup parsing logic of the RAG engine.

COMP101 - Advanced Topic in Systems 1

Credits: 3 ECTS Semester: Fall/Spring

This course covers the fundamentals and advanced topics required for modern computing systems. Topics include architecture design, parallel processing, and distributed network setups. Students are expected to complete a term project that demonstrates their understanding of Advanced Topic in Systems 1. Laboratory sessions run bi-weekly and require a passing grade.

  • Understand theoretical foundations of COMP101
  • Apply methodologies to solve complex engineering problems
  • Analyze existing systems and identify optimization points

Students are expected to complete a term project that demonstrates their understanding of Advanced Topic in Systems 65. Laboratory sessions run bi-weekly and require a passing grade.

  • Understand theoretical foundations of COMP165
  • Apply methodologies to solve complex engineering problems
  • Analyze existing systems and identify optimization points

Credits: 2 ECTS Semester: Fall/Spring

This course covers the fundamentals and advanced topics required for modern computing systems. Topics include architecture design, parallel processing, and distributed network setups. Students are expected to complete a term project that demonstrates their understanding of Advanced Topic in Systems 130. Laboratory sessions run bi-weekly and require a passing grade.

  • Analyze existing systems and identify optimization points

  • Analyze existing systems and identify optimization points

  • Understand theoretical foundations of COMP359
  • Apply methodologies to solve complex engineering problems
  • Analyze existing systems and identify optimization points

COMP424 - Advanced Topic in Systems 324

Credits: 6 ECTS Semester: Fall/Spring

This course covers the fundamentals and advanced topics required for modern computing systems. Topics include architecture design, parallel processing, and distributed network setups. Students are expected to complete a term project that demonstrates their understanding of Advanced Topic in Systems 324. Laboratory sessions run bi-weekly and require a passing grade.

  • Understand theoretical foundations of COMP424
  • Apply methodologies to solve complex engineering problems
  • Analyze existing systems and identify optimization points

COMP489 - Advanced Topic in Systems 389

Credits: 6 ECTS Semester: Fall/Spring

This course covers the fundamentals and advanced topics required for modern computing systems. Topics include architecture design, parallel processing, and distributed network setups. Students are expected to complete a term project that demonstrates their understanding of Advanced Topic in Systems 389. Laboratory sessions run bi-weekly and require a passing grade.

  • Understand theoretical foundations of COMP489
  • Apply methodologies to solve complex engineering problems
  • Analyze existing systems and identify optimization points
